data.title: Mexican Gold Mining Corp Completes $850,000 Private Placement
data.description:
Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Mexican Gold Corp ( (TSE:MEX) ) has provided an update.

Mexican Gold Mining Corp has successfully closed a non-brokered private placement, raising $850,000 through the issuance of 10 million units at $0.085 per unit. The proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, and the offering has been approved by the company’s board. An insider, John Anderson, participated in the offering, which was deemed a related party transaction but did not require a material change report due to its size relative to the company’s market capitalization. The offering is subject to final acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange, and certain restrictions apply to the exercise of warrants issued in the placement.

Spark’s Take on TSE:MEX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TSE:MEX is a Underperform.

Mexican Gold Corp faces significant challenges with no revenue generation and persistent financial losses, which severely impact its overall stock score. The recent private placement offers a temporary financial lifeline, but the company’s valuation remains unattractive with negative earnings. Technical indicators show mixed signals, with short-term momentum providing a glimmer of hope. Overall, the company’s precarious financial position significantly weighs down its stock score.

To see Spark’s full report on TSE:MEX stock, click here.

More about Mexican Gold Corp

Mexican Gold Mining Corp is a Canadian-based mineral exploration and development company focused on building long-term value through discoveries and strategic acquisitions of precious metals and copper projects in the Americas. The company is actively exploring and advancing the Las Minas Project in Veracruz State, Mexico, known for its under-explored skarn systems.

Average Trading Volume: 91,311

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$2.9M

For a thorough assessment of MEX stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: PMET Resources Advances Shaakichiuwaanaan Lithium Project with Positive Feasibility Study
data.description:

PMET Resources Inc ( (TSE:PMET) ) has shared an update.

PMET Resources Inc. has filed a technical report on its Shaakichiuwaanaan Project, highlighting a positive feasibility study for a large-scale lithium mining operation. The study reveals a significant mineral reserve and positions the project as one of the largest lithium pegmatite resources in the Americas, with potential for substantial production of spodumene concentrate. This development underscores the project’s importance in the global lithium market and its potential as a critical mineral powerhouse in North America.

data.title: NGEx Minerals Strengthens Financial Position and Advances Lunahuasi Exploration

The latest announcement is out from NGEx Minerals ( (TSE:NGEX) ).

NGEx Minerals Ltd. has reported its Q3 2025 results, highlighting the launch of its Phase 4 drill program at the Lunahuasi project in Argentina, following a successful Phase 3 campaign. The company has also completed a C$175 million oversubscribed private placement, which strengthens its financial position and supports further exploration activities. Additionally, NGEx Minerals has spun out an 80.1% ownership interest in LunR Royalties Corp. to its shareholders, which holds royalties on the company’s projects in Chile and Argentina. These developments position NGEx Minerals to expand its exploration efforts and potentially enhance its value creation through successful mineral discoveries.

data.title: Fluent Inc’s Earnings Call: Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Fluent Inc ((FLNT)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

Fluent Inc’s Recent Earnings Call: A Mixed Bag with Optimistic Future

The recent earnings call for Fluent Inc presented a mixed picture, with significant growth in commerce media solutions offset by declines in owned and operated revenue and overall consolidated revenue. Despite these challenges, the company remains optimistic about future growth and profitability, driven by new partnerships and strategic shifts.

Commerce Media Solutions Revenue Growth

Commerce media solutions were a standout performer in Fluent’s earnings report, with revenue in the third quarter growing over 80% year-over-year. This impressive growth increased its contribution to consolidated enterprise revenue from 16% in Q3 2024 to 40% in Q3 2025, highlighting the segment’s expanding importance to the company’s overall financial health.

New Partnerships and Expanding Market Share

Fluent announced several new partnerships, including collaborations with Databricks and Authentic Brands Group. These strategic alliances are expected to enhance Fluent’s market position in commerce media, providing a robust platform for future growth and increased market share.

Equity Raise Strengthens Financial Position

In a move to bolster its financial standing, Fluent completed a $10.3 million equity raise. This infusion of capital strengthens the company’s balance sheet, providing additional resources to fuel growth in its burgeoning commerce media solutions segment.

Expected Positive Adjusted EBITDA

Looking ahead, Fluent expects to achieve positive adjusted EBITDA in Q4 2025, with double-digit revenue growth anticipated in 2026. This optimistic forecast is driven by the continued expansion of commerce media solutions, which the company sees as a key driver of future profitability.

Decline in Owned and Operated Revenue

While commerce media solutions are on the rise, Fluent’s owned and operated revenue saw a significant decrease, dropping 52% year-over-year. The company expects this decline to continue into the fourth quarter, posing a challenge to overall revenue growth.

Consolidated Revenue Decrease

Fluent reported a total consolidated revenue of $47 million in 2025, down from $64.5 million the previous year. This significant decline underscores the challenges the company faces in stabilizing its overall revenue streams.

Adjusted EBITDA Loss

The company reported an adjusted EBITDA loss of $3.4 million in 2025, compared with a loss of $71,000 in 2024. This increase in losses highlights the financial pressures Fluent is under as it navigates its strategic pivot.

Advertiser Pricing and Budget Pullback

Fluent experienced some advertiser pricing and budget pullback in the latter part of Q3, which impacted revenue and gross profit. This trend poses a risk to future financial performance if it continues.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Fluent’s strategic pivot towards commerce media solutions is expected to drive future growth, with the company anticipating triple-digit revenue growth in this segment next year. Despite challenges such as timing delays and advertiser budget pullbacks, the company is optimistic about achieving double-digit gross profit growth in Q4 2025 and adjusted EBITDA profitability in 2026. The recent equity raise and new partnerships are seen as critical enablers of this growth trajectory.

In summary, Fluent Inc’s recent earnings call highlighted a mixed financial performance, with significant growth in commerce media solutions tempered by declines in other areas. However, the company’s strategic focus on commerce media, bolstered by new partnerships and an equity raise, provides a promising outlook for future growth and profitability.

data.title: Lifeward Ltd. Earnings Call: Optimism Amid Challenges
data.description:
Rewalk Robotics Ltd. ((LFWD)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Meet Your ETF AI Analyst

  • Discover how TipRanks' ETF AI Analyst can help you make smarter investment decisions
  • Explore ETFs TipRanks' users love and see what insights the ETF AI Analyst reveals about the ones you follow.

The recent earnings call for Lifeward Ltd. revealed a mixed sentiment, with notable operational improvements and strategic expansions, particularly in the ReWalk product line and European market access. Despite these positive strides, challenges persist, including declining revenue in the ATLAS G product line and increased operating expenses. However, the company remains optimistic about its transformation and future growth, supported by operational efficiencies and financial backing.

Record Quarter for ReWalk Placements

Lifeward Ltd. achieved another record quarter for ReWalk placements, specifically for Medicare beneficiaries. This marks the second consecutive record since the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) established their fee schedule in April 2024, highlighting the growing demand and acceptance of the ReWalk product line.

Operational Efficiencies Achieved

The company has successfully implemented operational efficiencies, resulting in a 16% reduction in quarterly cash burn and a 27% reduction in non-GAAP operating loss compared to the previous year. These improvements are pivotal in Lifeward Ltd.’s strategy to streamline operations and enhance profitability.

Expansion into European Market

With the CE Mark approval, Lifeward Ltd. has expanded its access to the European market. This expansion represents about 40% of their global addressable exoskeleton opportunity, positioning the company to capture significant market share in this region.

Improved Gross Profit Margins

Lifeward Ltd. reported GAAP gross profit of $2.7 million, or 43.7% of revenue in 2025, up from $2.2 million or 36.2% of revenue in 2024. This improvement was driven by lower production costs, reflecting the company’s focus on cost management and efficiency.

$3 Million Loan Agreement

The company announced the completion of a $3 million loan agreement with Oramed, which enhances its near-term liquidity and supports its ongoing transformation plans. This financial backing is crucial for sustaining operations and pursuing growth initiatives.

Decline in ATLAS G Product Revenue

Revenue from the ATLAS G product and services declined to $3.1 million in Q3 2025, down from $3.6 million in Q3 2024. This decrease is attributed primarily to timing factors and the quarterly revenue mix, indicating challenges in this segment.

Increased GAAP Operating Expenses

GAAP operating expenses increased to $5.9 million in 2025 from $5.4 million in 2024. This rise is largely due to a $2 million earn-out write-down recognized in the prior year, reflecting the company’s ongoing investment in growth and development.

Forward-Looking Guidance

During the earnings call, Lifeward Ltd. reaffirmed its full-year 2025 revenue expectations between $24 million and $26 million, with a projected non-GAAP net loss ranging from $12 million to $14 million. The company anticipates further reductions in operating loss as sales volumes grow, supported by a $3 million loan agreement with Oramed and ending the quarter with $2 million in cash.

In summary, Lifeward Ltd.’s earnings call reflected a cautiously optimistic outlook, with significant operational improvements and strategic expansions counterbalanced by challenges in certain product lines and increased expenses. The company’s commitment to transformation and growth, backed by financial support, positions it well for future success.
